examples: If participation and the culture are incongruous, employees are likely to perceive the participation process as manipulative and be negatively affected by it.
Such traditional methods seem incongruous in our technical age.
She was small and fragile and looked incongruous in an army uniform.
She sees nothing unusual or incongruous in combining her modern lifestyle with 2, 000-year-old remedies.
He said a big settlement without an admonition was "counterintuitive and incongruous, " he said.
